episode_name: Internal Kosovo war before NATO intervention
admin_units: Kosovo province (AP Kosovo and Metohija); violence concentrated in Drenica, Dukagjin/Metohija, Llap, Shala, Kaçanik, and border municipalities
theater_description: Province-wide insurgency/counterinsurgency inside Kosovo before NATO intervention. Fighting was concentrated and shifted across municipalities, but the relevant civilian denominator remains Kosovo rather than the whole FRY.
geometry_method_note: Use a Kosovo-province polygon rather than whole-country Serbia/FRY. A finer event polygon would be preferable for municipality-month work, but no clean province-wide direct-death split is available at high level.
military_area_note: Military denominator would need KLA and FRY/Serbian forces actually operating in Kosovo, not the whole Yugoslav armed forces.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator should be Kosovo residents physically present in Kosovo; internal displacement remained inside the theater, external flight reduced exposure only for those outside Kosovo.
split_reason: Belligerent structure and violence mode changed sharply after NATO intervention; pre-24 March 1999 counts are sourced and coded differently from the bombing period.
quality_note: Good contextual evidence, but high-level public sources do not cleanly separate direct killings from disappearances across the full pre-NATO episode.

episode_name: Kosovo inside the NATO intervention window
theater_description: Province-wide Kosovo theater during the NATO air campaign, including Serbian/Yugoslav ground operations, KLA operations, forced displacement, and NATO strikes that landed in Kosovo.
geometry_method_note: Province-wide polygon is justified because the main HLC/KMB mortality and displacement series for the bombing window are Kosovo-wide. Internal displacement stayed inside the theater; refugees who left Kosovo reduce the civilian denominator over time.
military_area_note: Core military denominator covers KLA and FRY/Serbian forces operating in or command-responsible for Kosovo. Off-theater NATO aircrew are excluded from the core denominator because a theater-specific person-month series is not available.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure is modeled as civilians physically present in Kosovo, not all FRY civilians. Refugee outflow is subtracted over time; internal displacement remains inside the denominator.
split_reason: This window has the strongest direct-death source coverage and a distinct province-wide displacement regime after 24 March 1999.
quality_note: Numerators are strong and registry-based, but denominator scenarios and some HLC series disagreements (especially non-Albanian civilian and military side counts) keep uncertainty material.

episode_name: NATO air war outside Kosovo (Serbia proper and Montenegro targets)
theater_description: NATO strikes on FRY targets outside Kosovo, including infrastructure, barracks, industrial sites, and urban areas in Serbia proper and Montenegro.
geometry_method_note: Whole-country denominators are forbidden here because the affected civilian area was a shifting target network rather than the whole population of Serbia or Montenegro.
military_area_note: A valid denominator would require target- or sortie-linked exposure by site and military assignment, not national force totals.
civilian_area_note: A valid civilian denominator would require target-area population polygons or repeated local exposure windows around strike sites.
split_reason: Outside-Kosovo NATO victims exist in named lists, but the geography is too heterogeneous to combine with the Kosovo province denominator.
quality_note: Victim counts are list-based, but denominator geometry is not yet defensibly reconstructed.
